Title: Polyhydroxybutyrate synthesis in Camelina: Towards coproduction of renewable feedstocks for bioplastics and fuels

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Visual selection of transgenic seeds by fluorescence microscopy
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DsRed was included as a constitutive visual marker in all constructs. 2. It enabled selection of transgenic Camelina seeds. 3. It was not tested as a metabolic determinant of PHB accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient-metabolism core gene; included only as transgene marker and should not be interpreted as causally affecting PHB metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of acetyl-CoA to form acetoacetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor for PHB monomer synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable plastic feedstock for renewable materials
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed cytosol; granules in cotyledon cells and vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse versus controlled environmental chamber conditions; seed-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic co-expression in Camelina seeds with metabolite quantification by GC after butanolysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to de novo PHB accumulation in seeds up to 4.5% with cytosolic pathway and up to 10.2% with ER-anchored PhaC pathway configuration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phaA from C. necator was part of the heterologous three-enzyme PHB pathway expressed in Camelina seeds. 2. Cytosolic pathway expression enabled substantial PHB accumulation in seeds. 3. In combination with ER-anchored PhaC, the pathway supported more stable multigenerational PHB production up to 10.2% seed weight.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component of a seed-targeted PHB engineering stack for co-production of polymer with oil in Camelina.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHB-producing lines showed narrower cotyledons, delayed seedling development, reduced oil content, and altered fatty acid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function was validated as part of a multigene bacterial pathway rather than individually dissected in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific soybean oleosin isoform A promoter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the direct monomer substrate for PHA synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable plastic feedstock for renewable materials
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed cytosol; granules in cotyledon cells and vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse versus controlled environmental chamber conditions; seed-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic co-expression in Camelina seeds with metabolite quantification by GC after butanolysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled PHB monomer supply and overall PHB accumulation when co-expressed with phaA and phaC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phaB from C. necator was included in the seed-expressed PHB pathway. 2. Its co-expression supported conversion of precursor to the PHB monomer unit in Camelina seeds. 3. Together with phaA and phaC, it enabled stable high PHB accumulation when PhaC was ER-anchored.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required enzyme for PHB engineering stacks in oilseeds.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated pathway expression correlated with narrower cotyledons, delayed seedling development, reduced oil content, and altered fatty acid profile.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as part of the integrated pathway, not through single-gene dissection in Camelina.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHA synthase; hybrid Pseudomonas oleovorans/Zoogloea ramigera enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Seed-specific soybean oleosin isoform A promoter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol or anchored to cytosolic face of endoplasmic reticulum in pMBXS763 configuration
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/stability determinant in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biodegradable plastic feedstock for renewable materials
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed cytosol; PHB granules in cotyledon cytosol and vacuoles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse versus controlled environmental chamber; ER-tail anchoring strategy using Arabidopsis cytochrome b5 isoform D sequence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparison of cytosolic versus ER-anchored pathway constructs across generations; GC-based PHB quantification; TEM localization of granules; polymer characterization by 1H NMR and GPC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ER-anchored PhaC gave more stable multigenerational PHB production and enabled up to 10.2% PHB in mature seeds with good viability
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. phaC catalyzes the polymerization step that directly generates PHB. 2. Cytosolic phaC supported PHB accumulation up to 4.5% seed weight but production was unstable across generations. 3. Fusion of phaC to an ER-anchoring tail yielded more stable PHB accumulation, reaching 10.2% seed weight under chamber conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target for improving plant PHB production; subcellular retargeting of PhaC is a useful design strategy to enhance stability and yield.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHB accumulation was associated with narrower cotyledons, delayed seedling development, reduced oil content, and altered fatty acid composition, though seed yield penalties were not statistically significant.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ER localization of PhaC was inferred from construct design and prior literature; it was not directly experimentally confirmed in this study.
